mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity markets to reprice earnings upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a monthly increase in industrial production reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike concerns, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a win rate below 45% or Rank IC consistently negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising household income and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS changes yield negative 20-day SPX returns in >58% of cases, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a collapse in construction demand and broader economic weakness, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: The threshold breach fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >50% of triggered months, or the signal shows no edge in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real rates turn negative and market expectations for aggressive central bank tightening increase, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The inflation-rate divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms economic acceleration, boosting corporate profit forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 readings above 2.5% in early-cycle phases fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a win rate below 48% or Rank IC consistently below 0.02.
